notes: Seasonal rules (flu, COVID) use schedule+season for campaign-window suppression. One-off lifetime rules (pneumococcal, shingles, RSV) use schedule:once. INTENTIONAL OMISSIONS: pertussis-in-pregnancy and RSV-in-pregnancy are not encoded — the engine has no per-pregnancy episode gate or gestational-age gate; a lifetime lookback cannot distinguish doses given in prior pregnancies.

rules.source: JCVI/UKHSA Green Book Chapter 19 / National flu immunisation programme letter 2026/27 (published 26 February 2026, DHSC/UKHSA/NHSE). No change to eligible cohorts for 2026/27.
rules.notes: DOUBLE-CHECK ELIGIBILITY: Status inferred from coded records only — vaccination given elsewhere may not appear. Asthma sub-criterion requires inhaled/systemic steroids or prior hospital admission (proxy via medication check). Carers, care home residents not detected. BMI reading may be out of date.

DOUBLE-CHECK ELIGIBILITY: As of 2025/26, clinical risk groups (diabetes, CHD, CKD, asthma) are NO LONGER eligible for COVID booster — only age 75+, care home residents, and immunosuppressed. Status inferred from coded records only — vaccination given elsewhere may not appear.

rules.source: UKHSA Green Book Chapter 25 (Pneumococcal) — single dose at age 65+; PCV20 (Prevenar 20) replaced PPV23 (Pneumovax 23) from December 2025 per JCVI advice; PGD valid from 17 December 2025
rules.notes: DOUBLE-CHECK ELIGIBILITY: One-off dose from age 65. Status inferred from coded records only — vaccination given elsewhere (or pre-registration) may not appear. From December 2025, PCV20 (Prevenar 20) has replaced PPV23 (Pneumovax 23) as the NHS adult pneumococcal vaccine — both coding terms are now included in statusTerms. Revaccination every 5 years applies ONLY to asplenia/splenic dysfunction/CKD and is NOT encoded here — check manually for those patients. At-risk under-65 cohort (Green Book ch 25 risk groups) intentionally not encoded.

rules.source: UKHSA Green Book Chapter 28a (Shingles) — phased programme from 1 Sept 2023: routine offer on turning 65 (cohort born on/after 1 Sept 1958) plus existing 70–79 cohort; Shingrix 2 doses 6–12 months apart
rules.notes: DOUBLE-CHECK ELIGIBILITY AND DOSES: Shingrix is a 2-dose course (6–12 months apart) — this chip shows GIVEN on ANY recorded shingles/zoster vaccination and CANNOT verify 2-dose completion; check dose count manually. People aged 68–69 who turned 65 before 1 Sept 2023 are NOT yet eligible and are correctly not flagged. Severely immunosuppressed 18+ pathway is NOT encoded (engine cannot combine age + clinical criteria in one clause) — assess manually. Historic Zostavax counts as given.

rules.source: UKHSA Green Book Chapter 27a (RSV) / JCVI advice June 2023 (initial programme) + NHS England expansion letter 1 April 2026 — routine offer at 75 with no upper age cap from 1 April 2026; care home residents eligible from 1 April 2026; single dose
rules.notes: DOUBLE-CHECK ELIGIBILITY: Single dose, no booster currently recommended. From 1 April 2026, all adults aged 75 and over are eligible (no upper age limit — the 75–79 restriction has been removed). ALL residents in care homes for older adults are also eligible regardless of age from 1 April 2026. RSV-in-pregnancy pathway (from 28 weeks) is intentionally NOT encoded — see pertussis omission note.

anyOf.label: Age 75+ (no upper age limit from 1 April 2026)
Care home resident (older adult care home, from 1 April 2026)
